steps.text: A low harmonic resonance vibrates through the metal floor plates, rising from the deep oceanic current below.

steps.speaker: Nova
steps.text: Do you feel that? The pressure... it's changing.
The air grows heavy, carrying the distant echo of a song long forgotten.
The squad retreats to regroup after the defeat.
Patch up, recharge shields, and consider buffing allies before re-engaging. Status items and defensive skills can swing the next encounter.
A security lock halts your progress.
Check your objectives or inventory—certain rooms need power restored or a keycard before they open.
Sensors flag movement beyond the doorway.
Clear nearby threats or prepare a pre-emptive strike—some exits stay sealed until the ambush is dealt with.

steps.speaker: Ollie
steps.text: Nova! There you are! We're late to meet Jed.

steps.speaker: Ollie
steps.text: Touch my name on your HUD, choose Talk, and let's catch up to him before he locks up.
The generator catches. Work lights snap on down the tunnel.
Heavy locks release farther down the mine.
Ellie carefully packs the shattered display into a crate for repair.
Maddie hands over the grinder, hopeful you can repair it.
Kasey reluctantly passes you the cracked projector lens.
All three broken units are back on Jed's bench.
Colony camera feeds cycle through wide, close, and tracking angles for visual calibration.
The view locks, drifts, then settles as the scene framing comes online.
Status captions pulse across the feed, translating colony alarms into something readable.
The caption layer holds long enough to read before the next warning takes its place.
Narration settles over the scene like a system log with a pulse.
Emergency color grading washes the image in warning reds and cold industrial blues.
The scene fades down until only the colony hum remains.
A white impact flare swallows the frame, then snaps back to the room.
A fractured memory cuts through the present, bright at the edges and gone too fast.
The frame narrows, focusing attention on the single choice left in front of Nova.
Trade Row opens into noise, heat, and a dozen quiet deals pretending to be legal.
Every visual layer stacks together: motion, captions, filters, and the pressure of the moment.
The narrator catches the quiet detail the alarms are too loud to notice.

The shift buzzer hits after four hours of sleep. First drill cage drops in forty minutes; absence penalties begin at the bell. Nova has one ration and a cutter that cannot make quota at factory settings.
The camera glides across the room, letting the damage tell the story first.
Dust, sparks, and tiny motes of resonance drift through the light.
A ring of pressure ripples outward, bending the air into visible sound.
Jed taps the bench twice, already watching to see whether Nova remembers the first rule: power off before hands in.
The cutter settles at a steady idle. Jed checks the gauge twice, then gives Nova a small nod.
The shattered display slides onto Jed's bench, another broken thing waiting for stubborn hands.
The grinder lands with a heavy clunk, smelling of dust, old grease, and useful trouble.
The cracked projector lens catches the work light, splitting it into tired little rainbows.
The frame jolts as something heavy hits nearby.
Motion streaks tear past the viewport as the launch rail takes hold.
The horizon tips sideways, making the corridor feel even less trustworthy.
The open square catches every sound at once: boots, vendors, distant machinery, and the low electric buzz of surveillance.
The visual effects layer blooms across the frame, then folds back into the scene.
The edges darken, pulling the eye toward the one detail that matters.
A hard transition sweeps the old room away and leaves the next danger waiting.
The view tightens slowly, closing the distance before anyone moves.
The camera pushes forward and tracks sideways, following the line of trouble through the room.

steps.speaker: Ollie
steps.text: Nova! There you are! We're late to meet Jed.

steps.speaker: Ollie
steps.text: Touch my name on your HUD, choose Talk, and let's catch up to him before he locks up.
Phase sweep: 87 kHz. Cold loop: 68%. Ground phase: 180 degrees. The three diagnostic bands lock.
The damaged cradle closes its circuit through cutter, suit, and operator. Heat spikes past the Cryo-Inductor's limit.
The Cryo-Inductor fuses. The Flux Liner dumps the neural discharge into the chamber floor and burns its sacrificial strip open.
Pain crosses Nova's teeth and left hand. Her suit clock skips from 14:03:22.4 to 14:03:22.9. A new protocol carrier appears in her nerve telemetry.
Nova releases the cutter. Her hand trembles. The Fork separates from the cradle while Harmony sensors flag a live Architect handshake.
The pod clears the launch rail on its own capacitors. The Chime opens the authorization interlock; its paired beacon takes the navigation bus.
Zeke's override fails. The forced route misses a permitted traffic corridor and drops the pod into a Sector 9 storm front.
Crosswind rolls the pod. The upper canopy tears one stabilizer free.

steps.speaker: Zeke
steps.text: Nova! Brace! The navigation bus is burning out—the route lock won't release!
The navigation console arcs and dies. Zeke hooks one arm through the frame as the hull starts tearing.
The shield rejects the pod, throwing it back into the atmosphere in a spinning descent toward the overgrown ruins of Sector 9.
The thrusters cut. The pod drops through the canopy and hits hard.
Nova plugs her datapad into the debugging port. Encrypted command blocks fill the terminal.
A hidden data block decrypts. The signature on the package is marked by standing waves and acoustic patterns, signed by the 'Chorus'.
The terminal confirms: PROTOCOL OVERRIDE BROADCAST. LOCAL ADMIN PRIVILEGES ACTIVE.
You boot up the discarded datapad. The battery is critically low, but the final text log is intact.
Log Entry: 'They sealed the doors from the outside. Said the collapse was a biohazard risk, but we only hit a pocket of hum.'
Log Entry: 'The resonance is beautiful. It doesn't sound like a machine. It sounds like currents in a deep ocean. I'm not afraid of the dark anymore.'
The screen flickers and goes dark permanently, leaving you in the cold silence of the mine.
SECTOR 9 - TWENTY YEARS EARLIER. Fresh Dominion braces are bolted across older Architect geometry.

steps.speaker: Lab PA
steps.text: SOURCE BEAST CONTAINMENT BREACH. FAR DOOR PRESSURE RISING.
The far doors buckle inward. Orion pulls a brass Chime from its signal cradle and locks it into the emergency tube.

steps.speaker: Orion
steps.text: Broadcast my identity. Draw it away.
The tube fires. The Chime's beacon moves into the dark, and the impact against the doors follows it.

steps.speaker: Orion
steps.text: Mute my remaining signal. Begin stasis.
The pod seals. Far outside, the Chime keeps transmitting.
Nova clamps the Flux Liner's ground strip to the floor bus. Cold loop: 68%. Governor: bypassed.

steps.speaker: Nova
steps.text: Bypass confirmed. One live sweep.
The cutter fires. Its phase sweep enters the buried old conduit under Jed's bench. The sector breaker trips hard enough to shake dust from the rafters.

steps.speaker: Jed
steps.text: Kill it!

steps.speaker: Nova
steps.text: It is off. That hum is not mine.
A terminal prints CUTTER FAULT 4C-117 and attaches operator NOVA VANCE. Deep below the shop, a chamber seal cracks.
Nova opens the Bridge alone. Heat climbs her arm and her knees buckle.

steps.speaker: Orion
steps.text: Close it. The Bridge connects; it does not make one body carry the circuit.
Zeke clips a ground lead to Nova's bracer. Gh0st checks the empty command channel, then offers his armored hand.

steps.speaker: Gh0st
steps.text: No command attached. Proceed.
Orion holds the counter-tone. The current divides across four willing contacts, and Zeke's wound steadies.

steps.speaker: Nova
steps.text: Link. Not merge. I get it.
The Ascended falls, but Vale's forced network remains active. Five protocol signatures align through the crew's practiced circuit and complete the Key's access pattern.

steps.speaker: Nova
steps.text: Ground lead failed. Zeke, manual bus. Gh0st, brace left. Orion, hold the counter-tone.
The interface rejects Zeke, so he holds the bare ground path by hand. Gh0st takes the force aimed at the crew. Orion keeps the counter-tone level.

steps.speaker: Orion
steps.text: The Key is open. Release root access when the corridor clears.
Nova opens a regional corridor, drops Vale's forced links, and releases root access. Her Source connection burns out as the network closes.
They fall together.
Morning in the Colony. One regional corridor stands open. Vale and Harmony are gone here; Dominion traffic still moves beyond the sector.
At Zeke's diner, Nova remembers Tideglass sand in her boots and Zeke complaining about salt in the filters. Her tuning hand trembles against the photograph.

steps.speaker: Zeke
steps.text: For record accuracy: I complained about corrosion. The humidity was an associated grievance.

steps.speaker: Gh0st
steps.text: You also threw a canteen at me. Accuracy: 94%.

steps.speaker: Nova
steps.text: The Source is gone. The thin whine stayed. I remember the rest.

steps.speaker: Orion
steps.text: Good. Now you can hear the wind.

steps.speaker: Nova
steps.text: Sounds like a good day. Fill me in on the rest?

steps.speaker: Zeke
steps.text: Pull up a chair. It is a long story.
STAR BORN
Created by the Starborn team. Thank you for playing.
